MLSSurface Class Reference

This class represents Moving Least Squares Surfaces. More...

#include <mls_surface.h>

List of all members.

Public Member Functions

 MLSSurface ()
 Default constructor.
 MLSSurface (int npoints, Vector3 *points)
 A construtor that creates an MLS surface instance defined by the array of vectors points, wich has npoints.
 ~MLSSurface ()
 Destructor.
Vector3 normal (const Vector3 &p)
 Normal estimation in the point specified.
float energy (const Vector3 &p, Vector3 n)
 Energy function associated to an MLS Surface. x represents the position and a represents the unoriented direction.
const int npoints () const
 Gets the number of points wich define the surface.
void npoints (int n)
 Sets the number of points wich define the surface.
const Vector3 * p () const
 Gets the set of points wich define the surface.
void p (Vector3 *points)
 Sets the set of points wich define the surface.
const float h () const
 Gets the smoothness parameter of the MLS surface.
void h (float hh)
 Sets the smothness parameter of the MLS surface. h low indicates a more local interpolation, h high indicates a global interpolation, creating a smooth surface.

Detailed Description

This class represents Moving Least Squares Surfaces.

Constructor & Destructor Documentation

MLSSurface::MLSSurface (  ) 

Default constructor.

MLSSurface::MLSSurface ( int  npoints,
Vector3 *  points 
)

A construtor that creates an MLS surface instance defined by the array of vectors points, wich has npoints.

The constructor creates a new copy of the array points, and assigns it to the member _p.

MLSSurface::~MLSSurface (  ) 

Destructor.

Member Function Documentation

Vector3 MLSSurface::normal ( const Vector3 &  point  )  [virtual]

Normal estimation in the point specified.

Explanation in "Approximating and Intersecting Surfaces from Points" Eurographics Symposiun on Geometry Processing 2003." Anderson Adamson and Marc Alexa. pg 232.

Implements ExtremalSurface.

float MLSSurface::energy ( const Vector3 &  x,
Vector3  a 
) [virtual]

Energy function associated to an MLS Surface. x represents the position and a represents the unoriented direction.

Function that increase with the distancy of the point cloud. It avoids errors during the optimization process. Reference: ALEXA M. BEHR J., COHEN-OR D.FLEISHMAN S. LEVIN D. SILVA C. T: Computing and rendering point set surfaces. IEEE Transaction on Visualization and Computer Graphics 9, 1 (2003).

Implements ExtremalSurface.

const int MLSSurface::npoints (  )  const [inline]

Gets the number of points wich define the surface.

void MLSSurface::npoints ( int  n  )  [inline]

Sets the number of points wich define the surface.

const Vector3* MLSSurface::p (  )  const [inline]

Gets the set of points wich define the surface.

void MLSSurface::p ( Vector3 *  points  )  [inline]

Sets the set of points wich define the surface.

const float MLSSurface::h (  )  const [inline]

Gets the smoothness parameter of the MLS surface.

void MLSSurface::h ( float  hh  )  [inline]

Sets the smothness parameter of the MLS surface. h low indicates a more local interpolation, h high indicates a global interpolation, creating a smooth surface.
